rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: layout_action.md contains an explicit transcription table listing R1..R4 and global inputs (normalized_threshold_score, remediation_budget_usd, O1..O3) with per-row citations to the embedded packet. The presence of the xlsx artifact is confirmed in actual outputs. Transcription values in the markdown match the embedded-packet values in the task trace.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Attach a workbook view or exported cell formulas (or a short 'method_selection' worksheet screenshot) to make the formula cells directly inspectable without requiring openpyxl.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: The observed_risk_score formula is documented in layout_action.md as the average of four min-max normalized components with explicit formulas for each component and references only to packet fields. Intermediate calculation table shows computed component values and observed_risk_score for each record consistent with the stated formulas.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Document explicit tie-breaking text in the decision table (e.g., how equality at boundaries is handled) to avoid ambiguity for boundary cases.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Margin is defined as 90 - observed_risk_score (normalized_threshold_score is documented as 90). Classification thresholds and tie-break rule (warn range 0..4.5 = 5% of 90) are stated. An explicit decision table lists pass/warn/fail for each record with row-level citations and shows R4 as control record with minimum margin 15.0.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide at least one worked example calculation cell formula (or screenshot) inside the spreadsheet for a combined-remediation case so verifiers can quickly recompute without needing openpyxl.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Remediation options O1..O3 are listed with costs and percent effects and a remediation-combination table enumerates costs, budget feasibility, post-remediation risk scores and margins for R4. The math for impacts (e.g., O2 reducing turn risk by 18% yielding R4 observed_risk_score 70.5 and margin 19.5) matches the stated reduction approach. A +/-5% sensitivity check is described and the worksheet claims no classification changes; expert-review flags are included.

rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Required artifacts present. layout_action.md includes a row-level evidence column citing embedded-packet fields for R1..R4. rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: In the workbook, include a compact 'method_selection' table showing side-by-side numeric examples of the alternative formulas (with the same record) so a reviewer can immediately reproduce and compare results without re-running sensitivity checks.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: layout_action.md states the observed_risk_score formula explicitly and documents it is packet-only (equal-weight normalized average). Alternatives are noted and needs_review flagged for the safe_margin inversion. Spreadsheet reportedly contains calculation worksheets and formulas (420 formula cells, Risk_Model sheet listed).

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Explicitly document tie-breaker logic in the Decision sheet for completeness (e.g., equality to thresholds) even if no record currently falls on a boundary.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Margin is reported vs normalized_threshold_score=90.0 in the action memo and classifications (pass/warn) appear in the Decision table with row-level citations. The sensitivity-triggered expert-review flag for R4 is documented. No boundary tie case was present; classification logic matches rubric rules.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Make the remediation-effect formulas explicit in the Remediation worksheet (e.g., show exact arithmetic that maps 'O3 improves normalized margin by 9%' into the observed_risk_score/margin cells) so a verifier can recompute post-remediation numbers without relying on memo text.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Remediation table enumerates costs and feasibility, checks combinations against remediation_budget_usd, and shows sensitivity worksheet results with an expert-review flag for R4. The chosen primary remediation (O3) and its cost are shown with budgetary checks and contingency combos. Ambiguous modeling choices (O3 interpreted as +9% normalized margin) are marked needs_review in the memo.
